title: FSSAI seizes 6,500 litres of suspected adulterated ghee in India
first_2_lines: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has dismantled an alleged inter-state network involved in the manufacture and distribution of suspected counterfeit ghee, seizing more than 6,500 litres of suspected adulterated products and related raw materials during coordinated operations in Delhi and Haryana. / The enforcement action was conducted by Central Food Safety Officers (CFSOs) under the guidance of Devesh Kumar Mahla, IPS, Director of the Northern Regional Office (NRO), FSSAI, in collaboration with local police authorities.

Major crackdown on adulterated ghee and oil in Pokaran; over 14,000kg seized
first_2_lines: JAISALMER: In a major action under the Rajasthan Governmentâs âShuddh Aahar Milawat Par Vaarâ campaign, authorities raided an illegal adulterated ghee and oil manufacturing unit in Pokaran late Sunday night and seized more than 14,000 kilograms of adulterated ghee and edible oil. / The operation is being described as the largest crackdown on the fake ghee and oil trade in Jaisalmer district so far.

title: FDA J&K Seizes 1,092 kg Suspected Fake Verka Ghee
first_2_lines: SRINAGAR, June 25 (KNS): The Jammu and Kashmir Food & Drugs Administration (FDA) has seized over 1,000 kg of suspected counterfeit 'Verka' ghee following raids across Jammu and Srinagar, officials said on Thursday. / An official told news agency Kashmir News Service (KNS), acting on a specific complaint, FDA food safety teams conducted inspections at multiple locations and seized 91 cases of the product – 55 from Jammu and 36 from Srinagar – bearing batch number F956210 with manufacturing date of May 11, 2026, and expiry of May 10, 2027.

Jalna Factory Raided For Making Fake Ghee, Rs 13 Lakh Stock Seized
first_2_lines: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has cracked down on an adulterated ghee manufacturing unit in Jalna, Maharashtra, seizing over Rs 13 lakh worth of fake products and initiating legal proceedings to ensure food safety. / Key Points

title: CCB unearths interstate racket selling adulterated Nandini ghee; 8,136 litres seized
first_2_lines: The Central Crime Branch (CCB) has dismantled a large interstate network involved in producing adulterated ghee in Tamil Nadu, packing it in fake Nandini-branded sachets and bottles, and distributing it across Bengaluru. / Four accused have been arrested, and 8,136 litres of adulterated ghee worth ₹1.26 crore have been seized.

Interstate fake Nandini ghee racket busted in Bengaluru; 4 held with goods worth Rs 1.26 cr
first_2_lines: Interstate fake Nandini ghee racket busted in Bengaluru; 4 held with goods worth Rs 1.26 cr / A CCB Special Investigation Squad officer said that the accused were manufacturing adulterated ghee in Tamil Nadu using coconut and palm oil.
